pairs.en: Wednesday, February 18, 2026 at 5 p.m. ET
pairs.zh: 2026 年 2 月 18 日星期三下午 5 点ET

pairs.en: CALL PARTICIPANTS
pairs.zh: 致电参与者

pairs.en: Chief Executive Officer — Dylan Field
pairs.zh: 首席执行官 — 迪伦·菲尔德

pairs.en: Chief Financial Officer — Praveer Melwani
pairs.zh: 首席财务官 — Praveer Melwani

pairs.en: Head of Investor Relations — Kate DeLeo
pairs.zh: 投资者关系主管 - Kate DeLeo

pairs.en: Revenue-- $304 million for the quarter, representing 40% growth year over year, and $1.06 billion for the full year, up 41%.
pairs.zh: 收入——季度收入 3.04 亿美元，同比增长 40%；全年收入 10.6 亿美元，同比增长 41%。

pairs.en: Net Dollar Retention Rate (Paid Customers $10K+ ARR)-- 136%, up five percentage points sequentially from Q3, and the highest rate in the past ten quarters.
pairs.zh: 净美元留存率（付费客户 10,000 美元+ ARR）——136%，比第三季度连续上升 5 个百分点，是过去 10 个季度的最高比率。

pairs.en: Gross Margin-- 86% for the quarter and 88% for the full year on a non-GAAP basis.
pairs.zh: 毛利率——按非公认会计原则计算，本季度为 86%，全年为 88%。

pairs.en: Non-GAAP Operating Margin-- 14% in the quarter; 12% for the full year, with non-GAAP operating income of $44 million for the quarter and $130 million for the year.
pairs.zh: 非 GAAP 运营利润率——本季度为 14%；全年增长 12%，非 GAAP 本季度营业收入为 4400 万美元，全年营业收入为 1.3 亿美元。

pairs.en: Adjusted Free Cash Flow Margin-- 13% for the quarter; adjusted free cash flow was $38 million, impacted by infrastructure investment, vendor payments timing, and a $25 million IP transfer tax related to the Weavee acquisition.
pairs.zh: 调整后自由现金流利润率——本季度为 13%；调整后的自由现金流为 3800 万美元，受到基础设施投资、供应商付款时间以及与 Weavee 收购相关的 2500 万美元知识产权转让税的影响。

pairs.en: Cash and Marketable Securities-- $1.7 billion at the end of the year.
pairs.zh: 现金和有价证券——年底为 17 亿美元。

pairs.en: Customer Growth ($10K+ ARR Tier)-- 951 net adds for $10,000+ ARR, and 143 net adds for $100,000+ ARR, which grew 46% year over year.
pairs.zh: 客户增长（10,000 美元以上 ARR 层级）——ARR 10,000 美元以上净增加 951 名，ARR 100,000 美元以上净增加 143 名，同比增长 46%。

pairs.en: Customer Growth ($1M+ ARR Tier)-- 67 paid customers surpassing $1 million in ARR, reflecting 68% year-over-year growth.
pairs.zh: 客户增长（100 万美元+ ARR 级别）——67 个付费客户的 ARR 超过 100 万美元，同比增长 68%。

pairs.en: International Revenue-- Grew 45% year over year; international customers were ~85% of monthly active users but contributed 54% of revenue.
pairs.zh: 国际收入——同比增长 45%；国际客户约占月活跃用户的 85%，但贡献了 54% 的收入。

pairs.en: Figma Make Weekly Active Users-- Increased over 70% sequentially from Q3 to Q4.
pairs.zh: Figma 每周活跃用户数量——从第三季度到第四季度连续增长超过 70%。

pairs.en: AI Feature Adoption-- Approximately 75% of paid customers with more than $10,000 in ARR consumed AI credits weekly as of the quarter-end.
pairs.zh: AI 功能采用——截至本季度末，大约 75% 的 ARR 超过 10,000 美元的付费客户每周消耗 AI 积分。

pairs.en: Outlook: Q1 2026 Revenue-- Guidance is $315 million to $317 million, representing 38% growth at the midpoint.
pairs.zh: 展望：2026 年第一季度收入——指导值为 3.15 亿至 3.17 亿美元，中间值增长 38%。

pairs.en: Outlook: FY 2026 Revenue-- Guidance is $1.366 billion to $1.374 billion, implying 30% growth at the midpoint.
pairs.zh: 展望：2026 财年收入——指导值为 13.66 亿美元至 13.74 亿美元，意味着中点增长 30%。

pairs.en: FY 2026 Non-GAAP Operating Income Guidance-- $100 million to $110 million, with a midpoint non-GAAP operating margin of 8%.
pairs.zh: 2026 财年非 GAAP 营业收入指导——1 亿至 1.1 亿美元，非 GAAP 营业利润率中点为 8%。

pairs.en: Figma Make Usage (Enterprise)-- Over 50% of paid customers spending $100,000+ in ARR used Figma Make weekly in Q4; nearly 60% of Makefiles in 2025 were created by non-designers.
pairs.zh: Figma Make 使用情况（企业）——在 ARR 中花费 100,000 美元以上的付费客户中，超过 50% 在第四季度每周使用 Figma Make；到 2025 年，近 60% 的 Makefile 是由非设计师创建的。

pairs.en: AI Image Editing Features-- Used more than 10 million times within weeks of launch in December 2025.
pairs.zh: AI 图像编辑功能——自 2025 年 12 月推出以来，几周内使用次数超过 1000 万次。

pairs.en: Praveer Melwani stated that sequential adjusted free cash flow declined in Q4, citing "continued investment in infrastructure, and AI, changes in the timing of vendor payments, and a one time $25 million IP transfer tax payment related to our acquisition of Weavie."
pairs.zh: Praveer Melwani 表示，第四季度经连续调整的自由现金流有所下降，理由是“对基础设施和人工智能的持续投资、供应商付款时间的变化，以及与我们收购 Weavie 相关的一次性 2500 万美元的知识产权转让税。”

pairs.en: Full-year non-GAAP operating income margin guidance for 2026 is 8% at the midpoint, down from 12% in 2025, attributed to accelerated AI and infrastructure investment.
pairs.zh: 2026 年全年非 GAAP 营业利润率指导值为中值 8%，低于 2025 年的 12%，归因于人工智能和基础设施投资的加速。

pairs.en: Stock-based compensation was elevated in 2025 due to IPO-related expenses and one-time vesting events, but management expects improvement in proportion to revenue going forward.
pairs.zh: 由于 IPO 相关费用和一次性归属事件，股票薪酬将在 2025 年提高，但管理层预计未来收入比例将有所改善。

pairs.en: Figma(FIG+9.19%)delivered a quarterly revenue of $304 million, achieving record annual revenue of $1.06 billion and acceleration in growth rates, as called out in prepared remarks. Customer engagement deepened, demonstrated by an over 70% sequential increase in Figma Make weekly active users and the adoption of new AI consumption models, with 75% of enterprise customers using AI credits weekly. Strategic expansion in international markets and the successful integration of eight product launches—including AI-native features—supported outperformance in net new customer adds, especially at higher ARR tiers. Management reiterated a disciplined approach to scaling, guiding to revenue growth of 30% for 2026 and a deliberate reduction in operating margin to fund continued AI innovation and infrastructure. The company referenced March as the inflection point for monetizing both seats and credits, indicating an anticipated shift in the revenue model not reflected in historical financials.
pairs.zh: Figma (FIG+9.19%) 季度收入达 3.04 亿美元，年度收入达到创纪录的 10.6 亿美元，并且增长率加快，正如准备好的讲话中所指出的那样。客户参与度不断加深，Figma Make 每周活跃用户环比增长超过 70%，以及新的 AI 消费模式的采用，其中 75% 的企业客户每周使用 AI 积分。国际市场的战略扩张以及八个产品发布的成功整合（包括人工智能原生功能）支持了新客户净增长的优异表现，尤其是在较高的 ARR 级别。管理层重申了严格的扩展方法，指导 2026 年收入增长 30%，并有意降低营业利润率，为持续的人工智能创新和基础设施提供资金。该公司将三月份视为席位和积分货币化的拐点，这表明收入模式的预期转变并未反映在历史财务数据中。

pairs.en: Dylan Field emphasized a strategy of "as models get better, Figma gets better," clarifying ongoing investments to advance AI-driven workflows and position the platform as central to design and development.
pairs.zh: Dylan Field 强调了“随着模型变得更好，Figma 也变得更好”的战略，明确了为推进人工智能驱动的工作流程而进行的持续投资，并将该平台定位为设计和开发的核心。

pairs.en: Praveer Melwani described the new hybrid monetization model—combining seat and credit consumption—as an area of evolving revenue predictability, with initial telemetry and add-on pack structures designed to balance pay-as-you-go bursts and contracted commitments.
pairs.zh: Praveer Melwani 将新的混合货币化模型（结合席位和信用消费）描述为收入可预测性不断发展的领域，其初始遥测和附加包结构旨在平衡即用即付突发和合同承诺。

pairs.en: Expansion in enterprise segments was reinforced by partnerships such as GitHub, which leverages Figma's DevMode MCP to connect design and development workflows, and a near doubling of hyperscaler seat footprints, including significant adoption among product managers.
pairs.zh: GitHub 等合作伙伴关系加强了企业细分市场的扩张，该合作伙伴利用 Figma 的 DevMode MCP 连接设计和开发工作流程，以及超大规模的席位几乎翻倍，包括产品经理的大量采用。

pairs.en: Management highlighted a continued shift in customer usage from traditional designers to expanded teams, with nearly 60% of Makefiles created by non-designers, signaling broader platform utility and the blurring of functional roles across product, engineering, and design.
pairs.zh: 管理层强调，客户使用情况不断从传统设计师向扩大的团队转变，近 60% 的 Makefile 由非设计师创建，这标志着更广泛的平台实用性以及产品、工程和设计之间功能角色的模糊。

pairs.en: The acquisition of Weavee broadens Figma's capabilities in AI-driven creative workflows for image, video, and motion generation, demonstrated by use cases at scale such as NVIDIA's keynote preparations.
pairs.zh: 收购 Weavee 拓宽了 Figma 在 AI 驱动的图像、视频和动作生成创意工作流程方面的能力，NVIDIA 的主题演讲准备等大规模用例就证明了这一点。

pairs.en: INDUSTRY GLOSSARY
pairs.zh: 行业术语

pairs.en: MCP (Model Connect Platform): Figma's integration layer linking design files and code, enabling bidirectional workflows between development and design environments.
pairs.zh: MCP（模型连接平台）：Figma 的集成层链接设计文件和代码，支持开发和设计环境之间的双向工作流程。

pairs.en: DevMode MCP: Specific to development workflows, allows users to pull code and design context between Figma and agentic coding tools.
pairs.zh: DevMode MCP：特定于开发工作流程，允许用户在 Figma 和代理编码工具之间提取代码和设计上下文。

pairs.en: Figma Make: Figma's AI-powered tool for rapid prototyping, open to designers and non-designers, supporting iterative creative processes and automation.
pairs.zh: Figma Make：Figma 的人工智能驱动工具，用于快速原型设计，向设计师和非设计师开放，支持迭代创意流程和自动化。

pairs.en: AI Credits: Metered units enabling customers to access AI features within Figma products; monetization is shifting from seat-based to a hybrid seat/consumption model.
pairs.zh: AI 积分：计量单位使客户能够访问 Figma 产品中的 AI 功能；货币化正在从基于座位的模式转向混合座位/消费模式。

pairs.en: Governance Plus: Figma's enterprise add-on for compliance and centralized governance of design assets and workflows.
pairs.zh: Governance Plus：Figma 的企业附加组件，用于设计资产和工作流程的合规性和集中治理。

pairs.en: Operator:Well, good day everyone and welcome to the Figma, Inc. Q4 2025 Earnings Call. Today's call is being recorded. If you have a question today, please press 1 on your telephone keypad. I would now like to turn the conference over to Mr. Brendan Mulligan. Please go ahead.
pairs.zh: 接线员：大家好，欢迎参加 Figma, Inc. 2025 年第四季度财报电话会议。今天的通话正在录音。如果您今天有疑问，请按电话键盘上的 1。现在我想将会议交给布伦丹·穆里根先生。请继续。

pairs.en: Kate DeLeo:Good afternoon, and thank you for joining us on today's conference call to discuss Figma, Inc.'s results for the 2025. On the call, have Dylan Field, Figma's cofounder and chief executive officer. And Praveer Melwani, our chief financial officer. During the course of today's call, we may make forward looking statements. Including but not limited to, statements regarding our guidance, and future financial performance, market demand, product development, growth prospects, business strategies and plans, partnerships, ability to attract and retain customers, ability to compete effectively. These forward looking statements are based on management's current views and assumptions. And should not be relied upon as of any subsequent date. And we disclaim any obligation to update any forward looking statements.
pairs.zh: Kate DeLeo：下午好，感谢您参加今天的电话会议，讨论 Figma, Inc. 2025 年的业绩。参加电话会议的有 Figma 联合创始人兼首席执行官迪伦·菲尔德 (Dylan Field)。还有我们的首席财务官 Praveer Melwani。在今天的电话会议中，我们可能会做出前瞻性陈述。包括但不限于有关我们的指导、未来财务业绩、市场需求、产品开发、增长前景、业务战略和计划、合作伙伴关系、吸引和留住客户的能力、有效竞争的能力的声明。这些前瞻性陈述基于管理层当前的观点和假设。并且在任何后续日期均不应依赖。我们不承担更新任何前瞻性陈述的义务。

pairs.en: Actual results may vary materially from today's statements. Information concerning our risks, uncertainties, and other factors that could cause results to differ from these forward looking statements included in our filings with the SEC. Including our annual report on Form 10 ks for the year ended 12/31/2025. Our discussion today will include certain non GAAP financial measures. These non GAAP financial measures should be considered in addition to, not as a substitute for or in isolation from GAAP measures. Our non GAAP measures exclude the effect of our GAAP results of stock based compensation and certain other items. Reconciliations of non GAAP financial measures to comparable GAAP measures can be found in our press release accompanying this call.
pairs.zh: 实际结果可能与今天的声明存在重大差异。有关我们的风险、不确定性和其他可能导致结果与我们向 SEC 提交的文件中包含的前瞻性声明不同的因素的信息。包括截至 2025 年 12 月 31 日的年度 10 ks 表格年度报告。我们今天的讨论将包括某些非公认会计准则财务指标。这些非公认会计原则财务措施应作为公认会计原则措施的补充，而不是替代或独立于公认会计原则措施。我们的非公认会计原则衡量指标排除了基于股票的薪酬和某些其他项目的公认会计原则结果的影响。非公认会计准则财务指标与可比公认会计准则财务指标的调节表可以在我们随本次电话会议发布的新闻稿中找到。

pairs.en: Which is posted to our website. I would now like to turn the conference call over to Dylan. Hi, everyone, and thank you for joining today's call.
pairs.zh: 这已发布到我们的网站上。我现在想把电话会议转给迪伦。大家好，感谢您参加今天的电话会议。

pairs.en: Dylan Field:2025 was a massive year for Figma. And the fourth quarter was our best quarter yet. I'll share a few highlights. We delivered $304,000,000 in revenue last quarter. This represents an accelerated year over year growth rate for the quarter of 40%. Our net dollar retention rate for customers with more than $10,000 in ARR also grew five percentage points quarter over quarter to 136%. And we generated cash. With a non GAAP operating margin of 14%. And an adjusted free cash flow margin of 13%. Ending the year with $1,700,000,000 of cash equivalents, and marketable securities. Our growth and momentum show that our strategy is working As AI gets better, Figma gets better. And we're shipping faster than ever.
pairs.zh: Dylan Field：2025 年对于 Figma 来说是重要的一年。第四季度是我们迄今为止最好的季度。我将分享一些亮点。上季度我们实现了 3.04 亿美元的收入。这意味着本季度同比增长率加快了 40%。对于 ARR 超过 10,000 美元的客户，我们的净美元保留率也环比增长了 5 个百分点，达到 136%。我们产生了现金。非 GAAP 营业利润率为 14%。调整后的自由现金流率为 13%。年底现金等价物和有价证券为 1,700,000,000 美元。我们的增长和势头表明我们的战略正在发挥作用 随着人工智能变得更好，Figma 也变得更好。而且我们的发货速度比以往任何时候都快。

pairs.en: In 2025, we expanded from four to eight products, and launched over 200 features. Including new AI native functionality. This momentum reflects amazing execution by our team. Thank you, Figmates. We've carried that same pace and product velocity into 2026. In fact, accelerating. Just yesterday, we launched the ability to bring work from Cloud Code directly into Figma. Let me show you how it works. Let's say I'm a developer building an app that looks like this. Many developers using AI start in Cloud Code. The terminal is familiar, fast, and powerful. So now I've brought this app to a decent place. But if I want to make it truly stand out, it needs to be excellent.
pairs.zh: 2025 年，我们的产品从四种扩展到八种，并推出了 200 多项功能。包括新的 AI 原生功能。这种势头反映了我们团队惊人的执行力。谢谢你，Figmates。到 2026 年，我们仍将保持同样的步伐和产品速度。事实上，正在加速。就在昨天，我们推出了将 Cloud Code 中的工作直接引入 Figma 的功能。让我向您展示它是如何工作的。假设我是一名开发人员，正在构建一个如下所示的应用程序。许多使用 AI 的开发人员都是从 Cloud Code 开始的。该终端熟悉、快速且功能强大。所以现在我已经把这个应用程序带到了一个不错的地方。但如果我想让它真正脱颖而出，它就必须非常出色。

pairs.en: I need to think about the ways to make it awesome. And that's where things get interesting. Part of me wants to keep polishing. Tweaking spacing, adjusting colors, refining every last detail, and you can see I've been painstakingly prompting AI. Trying to nudge it towards perfection. But what I really need to do is step back and explore different possibilities. I need to see the big picture. To bring in the smartest people on my team, and push toward bolder ideas. That's what design is about. Design is about giving people the freedom to explore. It's about asking what if. It's about seeing the bigger picture and pursuing the best possible solution.
pairs.zh: 我需要考虑如何让它变得很棒。这就是事情变得有趣的地方。我的一部分想要继续打磨。调整间距、调整颜色、完善每一个细节，你可以看到我一直在煞费苦心地促进人工智能的发展。试图将其推向完美。但我真正需要做的是退后一步，探索不同的可能性。我需要看到大局。引进团队中最聪明的人，并推动更大胆的想法。这就是设计的意义所在。设计就是给予人们探索的自由。这是关于问如果。这是为了看到更大的前景并追求最佳的解决方案。

pairs.en: And it's hard to do that when you're exploring one idea at a time in a linear fashion with one screen, one prompt, alone in a terminal. So we're changing that. We're making design accessible even from Cloud Code. And I can do that by simply typing send this app
pairs.zh: 当您在终端中仅用一个屏幕、一个提示以线性方式一次探索一个想法时，很难做到这一点。所以我们正在改变这一点。我们甚至可以通过 Cloud Code 进行设计。我只需输入“发送此应用程序”即可做到这一点

pairs.en: Kate DeLeo:to
pairs.zh: 凯特·德莱奥：到

pairs.en: Dylan Field:Figma. And when I open Figma, you can see the screen right there. These are fully editable design layers. I can adjust spacing, color, layout directly. Which is much faster than all that prompting that I was doing earlier. So now let's go back to the app. I can capture any part of it and I can send it to Figma. Maybe I want to go to this specific state. So I can iterate on it more. Or perhaps I just want this card. Because I'd like to explore how to make it better.
pairs.zh: 迪伦·菲尔德：Figma。当我打开 Figma 时，你可以看到屏幕就在那里。这些是完全可编辑的设计图层。我可以直接调整间距、颜色、布局。这比我之前所做的所有提示要快得多。现在让我们回到应用程序。我可以捕捉它的任何部分，然后将其发送到Figma。也许我想去这个特定的状态。所以我可以更多地迭代它。或者也许我只是想要这张卡。因为我想探索如何让它变得更好。

pairs.en: In seconds, I can extract the key parts of the experience, and send them right over to the shared canvas in Figma Design where my team and I can explore freely. And you can already see my teammates jumping in. Greg is exploring a completely different look and feel. It's bold and unexpected. Anna is mapping the user journeys and identifying gaps in the experience. Now we're not just polishing one idea. We are exploring many,
pairs.zh: 在几秒钟内，我就可以提取体验的关键部分，并将它们直接发送到 Figma Design 中的共享画布，我和我的团队可以在那里自由探索。你已经可以看到我的队友们加入进来。格雷格正在探索一种完全不同的外观和感觉。这是大胆且出乎意料的。Anna 正在绘制用户旅程并找出体验中的差距。现在我们不仅仅是完善一个想法。我们正在探索很多，

pairs.en: Operator:together.
pairs.zh: 接线员：一起。

pairs.en: Dylan Field:I can build on their ideas using direct manipulation or even prompt AI for variations. What you're seeing is the power of design in this infinite canvas. The ability to bring everyone together on my team. To explore and riff on divergent directions. To refine ideas with precision and speed, of direct manipulation. Now I can just use my hands to make edits. And I have the ability to zoom out and have the bird's eye view of what's going on and compare things side by side. And once we've explored, we've aligned, we've landed on the best solution, we can simply go back to Cloud Code and use our MCP server to bring these designs directly back into code.
pairs.zh: Dylan Field：我可以通过直接操作甚至提示人工智能进行变化来构建他们的想法。您在这片无限画布中看到的是设计的力量。将团队中的每个人聚集在一起的能力。探索和重复不同的方向。通过直接操作，精确、快速地完善想法。现在我可以用手进行编辑。我能够缩小范围，鸟瞰正在发生的事情并并排比较。一旦我们进行了探索、调整、找到了最佳解决方案，我们就可以简单地返回 Cloud Code 并使用我们的 MCP 服务器将这些设计直接带回到代码中。

pairs.en: Cloud Code to Figma is one example of how we're making it easier for teams to go from code the Figma Canvas with a lot more to come. And, of course, this builds on our existing dev mode m c p, which allows users to go from Canvas to code. We're excited about what we can do with additional partners via MCP to create a better round trip between design and production. Wherever you start. And for many of our users, that work starts Figma Make. Either as a rough idea, a detailed PRD, or an existing Figma design. And in Q4, usage of Figma makes surged. Weekly active users of FigmaMake grew over 70% quarter over quarter.
pairs.zh: Cloud Code to Figma 是我们如何让团队更轻松地从 Figma Canvas 进行代码转换的示例之一，未来还有更多功能。当然，这建立在我们现有的开发模式 m c p 的基础上，它允许用户从 Canvas 转到代码。我们很高兴能够通过 MCP 与其他合作伙伴一起创造更好的设计和生产之间的往返过程。无论你从哪里开始。对于我们的许多用户来说，这项工作始于Figma Make。无论是作为一个粗略的想法、一个详细的 PRD，还是一个现有的 Figma 设计。第四季度，Figma 的使用量激增。FigmaMake 的每周活跃用户环比增长超过 70%。

pairs.en: And as of Q4, over 50% of paid customers spending more than $100,000 in ARR we're building in Figma Make on a weekly basis. Figma Make has also unlocked new audiences and new use cases. In fact, of all Figma Makefiles created in 2025, nearly 60% were created by nondesigners. We're talking developers, PMs, marketers, and others inside the company broadly. Let me share two stories from our customers that show how teams are adopting Figma Make. For the design and product teams at Cisco, deciding what to build and how to build it is a constant challenge. To move faster, they align by making. Moving between Figma design and Figma make.
pairs.zh: 截至第四季度，超过 50% 的付费客户每周在 Figma Make 中的 ARR 支出超过 100,000 美元。Figma Make 还解锁了新的受众和新的用例。事实上，在 2025 年创建的所有 Figma Makefile 中，近 60% 是由非设计师创建的。我们谈论的是开发人员、产品经理、营销人员和公司内部的其他人。让我分享两个来自客户的故事，展示团队如何采用 Figma Make。对于思科的设计和产品团队来说，决定构建什么以及如何构建是一个持续的挑战。为了更快地行动，他们通过制造来对齐。在 Figma 设计和 Figma 制造之间切换。

pairs.en: Designers' NPMs often work in the same make file, passing ideas back and forth. PMs laying forth the broad strokes, designers refining them in real time. That speed is grounded in a strong design system foundation in Figma. Where teams work from a shared set of standards. Make templates kick off projects, and can be adjusted to suit all types of purposes, increasing throughput across everything the team builds. From interactive research readouts to early stage product explorations, even custom internal tools. Building on that foundation, a newly formed agentic design ops function uses Make to explore AI native workflows often starting from static design files and turning them into interactive simulations.
pairs.zh: 设计人员的 NPM 经常在同一个 make 文件中工作，来回传递想法。产品经理制定总体思路，设计师实时完善它们。这种速度是建立在 Figma 强大的设计系统基础之上的。团队按照一套共享的标准工作。让模板启动项目，并且可以进行调整以适应所有类型的目的，从而提高团队构建的所有内容的吞吐量。从交互式研究读数到早期产品探索，甚至定制内部工具。在此基础上，新成立的代理设计操作功能使用 Make 来探索 AI 原生工作流程，通常从静态设计文件开始，并将其转变为交互式模拟。

pairs.en: With demo now available through the Cisco App Store, many engineers who previously had view only access have now adopted dev mode. Improving speed and efficiency. By working directly inside of Figma. Together, these workflows form a continuous system in Figma. Bringing design craft, engineering, and automation into one connected flow. We found that for many teams using Figma Make, speed becomes a compounding advantage. At FlexSport, teams use Figma Make to solve company problems faster. Every year, they bring the top 150 leaders of the company together. This year, they added a hackathon with this challenge. Stop coming up with reasons to choose Flexport.
pairs.zh: 现在可以通过思科应用商店提供演示，许多以前只能查看访问权限的工程师现在已经采用了开发模式。提高速度和效率。直接在 Figma 内部工作。这些工作流程共同构成 Figma 中的一个连续系统。将设计工艺、工程和自动化整合到一个互联的流程中。我们发现，对于许多使用 Figma Make 的团队来说，速度成为了复合优势。在 FlexSport，团队使用 Figma Make 更快地解决公司问题。每年，他们都会将公司的 150 名顶尖领导者聚集在一起。今年，他们为此挑战增加了一场黑客马拉松。停止寻找选择 Flexport 的理由。

pairs.en: Instead, you have 24 hours to solve one of the reasons why buyers don't choose FlexBoard. The competition run like a March Madness bracket had basically every team pitching their solution to one of these problems using Figma Make. Showing working apps within a day. One of the winners completely redid onboarding of factories to the platform. Solving a long time challenge. That idea is shipping to customers within the next few weeks. Another winning team used AI to process transcripts, of customer conversations and then fed that data into Figma Make, to automatically create custom diagrams. These diagrams made incredibly easy for sales to contrast before FlexSport and after FlexSport worlds for the customer's supply chain.
pairs.zh: 相反，您有 24 小时的时间来解决买家不选择 FlexBoard 的原因之一。比赛就像疯狂三月的比赛一样，基本上每个团队都使用 Figma Make 提出解决方案来解决其中一个问题。显示一天内运行的应用程序。其中一位获胜者完全重新设计了工厂到该平台的登录流程。解决长期挑战。这个想法将在未来几周内交付给客户。另一个获胜团队使用人工智能处理客户对话的笔录，然后将数据输入 Figma Make，自动创建自定义图表。这些图表使销售人员能够非常轻松地对比客户供应链在 FlexSport 世界之前和之后的情况。

pairs.en: Figma Make is the preferred tool not only for the design team at FlexSport, but also for an even bigger transformation that is now underway. Moving from a document culture to a rapid prototyping culture. That solves problems faster. As the Flexport CEO told us, the teams that do that with me are the teams that are doing really well. We're especially excited to see how Figma make is driving meaningful cross platform adoption. In Q4, over 80% of Figma's makes weekly active users on full seats also use Figma Design. And to us, that means we need to go beyond features we've already launched.
pairs.zh: Figma Make 不仅是 FlexSport 设计团队的首选工具，也是目前正在进行的更大转型的首选工具。从文档文化转向快速原型文化。这样可以更快地解决问题。正如 Flexport 首席执行官告诉我们的那样，与我一起做到这一点的团队都是做得非常好的团队。我们特别高兴地看到 Figma make 如何推动有意义的跨平台采用。在第四季度，超过 80% 的 Figma 每周活跃用户也使用 Figma Design。对我们来说，这意味着我们需要超越已经推出的功能。

pairs.en: For example, the ability to copy UI generated fame and make as layers into Figma Design or more recently, the ability to embed Make files as prototypes on the Figma Design canvas. These are great but we have an opportunity to drive toward more integrated capabilities that bring these services even closer together. But going from code to Canvas is only one part of the story. We're also focused on completing the loop, and helping teams go from design to production as well. When this happens, we want Workstar and Figma to flow easily into the tools developers use every day.
pairs.zh: 例如，能够将 UI 生成的名气和 make 作为图层复制到 Figma Design 中，或者最近能够将 Make 文件作为原型嵌入 Figma Design 画布上。这些都很棒，但我们有机会推动更集成的功能，使这些服务更加紧密地结合在一起。但从代码到 Canvas 只是故事的一部分。我们还专注于完成循环，并帮助团队从设计到生产。当这种情况发生时，我们希望 Workstar 和 Figma 能够轻松融入开发人员日常使用的工具中。

pairs.en: With the DevMode MCP, which we launched last year, teams can pull design and code based context built in Figma into the preferred agentic coding tools. Customers like GitHub have told us that DevModeMCP is a game changer. GitHub uses Figma to evolve and ship primer, their design system. Where even small updates can affect more than 7,400 design tokens and tens of thousands of lines of code. And at that scale, tight coordination between design and engineering is essential. GitHub uses Figma's MCB server and CodeConnect, to surface real, production design system code directly in Figma. Each component is linked to its canonical implementation. Keeping design and engineering aligned. Changes can be validated early.
pairs.zh: 借助我们去年推出的 DevMode MCP，团队可以将 Figma 中内置的基于设计和代码的上下文拉入首选代理编码工具中。GitHub 等客户告诉我们，DevModeMCP 是一个游戏规则改变者。GitHub 使用 Figma 来发展和发布他们的设计系统 Primer。即使是很小的更新也会影响超过 7,400 个设计令牌和数万行代码。在这种规模下，设计和工程之间的紧密协调至关重要。GitHub 使用 Figma 的 MCB 服务器和 CodeConnect，直接在 Figma 中显示真实的生产设计系统代码。每个组件都与其规范实现相关联。保持设计和工程的一致性。可以尽早验证更改。

pairs.en: Before they cascade across thousands of tokens and components. Code Connect enables GitHub Copilot agents to generate against authoritative components. Improving accuracy and consistency from the start. But once required hours of back and forth during handoff, can now move forward in just minutes. Beyond using Figma internally, GitHub is also partnering with Figma. At the platform level. As a key partner in the GitHub MCP registry, Figma makes its MCP server discoverable and ready to power AI assisted workflows for developers using the GitHub product. While velocity is critical, the best product teams are not defined by speed alone. You can go really fast and still get to the wrong place. These teams are also defined by their craft.
pairs.zh: 在它们级联数千个令牌和组件之前。Code Connect 使 GitHub Copilot 代理能够针对权威组件进行生成。从一开始就提高准确性和一致性。但以前在交接过程中需要几个小时来回，现在只需几分钟即可向前推进。除了在内部使用 Figma 之外，GitHub 还与 Figma 合作。在平台层面。作为 GitHub MCP 注册中心的重要合作伙伴，Figma 使其 MCP 服务器可被发现，并准备好为使用 GitHub 产品的开发人员提供人工智能辅助工作流程。虽然速度至关重要，但最好的产品团队并不仅仅由速度来定义。你可能走得很快，但仍然会到达错误的地方。这些团队也由他们的技术来定义。

pairs.en: And in a world where software is growing exponentially, design, craft, and point of view are what makes the best products stand apart. But delivering high craft creative work often means stitching together multiple tools each optimized for a particular task. We're working to bring more of these advanced capabilities directly into Figma. So teams can spend less time wrangling all these different tools more time designing incredible products and staying in flow state. One way we've done this is through our AI image editing capabilities. Which we significantly enhanced with a new set of updates in December 2025. In just a matter of weeks, these AI image editing capabilities were used more than 10,000,000 times.
pairs.zh: 在软件呈指数级增长的世界中，设计、工艺和观点是最好的产品脱颖而出的关键。但交付高工艺创意作品通常意味着将多个工具拼接在一起，每个工具都针对特定任务进行了优化。我们正在努力将更多先进功能直接引入 Figma。因此，团队可以花更少的时间争论所有这些不同的工具，更多的时间设计令人难以置信的产品并保持流畅状态。我们实现这一目标的方法之一是通过我们的人工智能图像编辑功能。我们在 2025 年 12 月通过一组新的更新显着增强了这一功能。在短短几周内，这些 AI 图像编辑功能的使用次数就超过 10,000,000 次。

pairs.en: More recently, we launched new vector functionality in FigmaDraw. Vectorize, uses AI to transform simple images like a hand drawn sketch into dynamic vector illustrations. That you can then tweak, refine, and scale in Figma. As one user put it, this kind of work used to be painful, and now it's a click. It's clear that our users crave more ways to do their creative work in Figma. Which brings me to our Q4 acquisition of Weavee. Now from Moweave. Figma Weave's AI image, video, animation, and motion generation alongside precise creative control expands the creative work possible in Figma.
pairs.zh: 最近，我们在 FigmaDraw 中推出了新的矢量功能。Vectorize，使用 AI 将简单图像（如手绘草图）转换为动态矢量插图。然后您可以在 Figma 中进行调整、细化和缩放。正如一位用户所说，这种工作以前很痛苦，现在只需点击一下即可。很明显，我们的用户渴望有更多方式在 Figma 中进行创意工作。这让我想到了我们第四季度对 Weavee 的收购。现在来自 Moweave。Figma Weave 的 AI 图像、视频、动画和动作生成以及精确的创意控制扩展了 Figma 中的创意工作。

pairs.en: Customers have told us they love how Figma Weave helps them enhance their creative process by bringing craft to everything from intricate compositions to show stopping stage visuals. One example of this is NVIDIA. For the NVIDIA CS Keynote, the team set out to create a high fidelity group shot of 20 unique robots. For the massive 12 k Keynote screen. For a moment of this incredible scale they needed a flexible workflow. That allowed for rapid iteration without rebuilding the entire scene. The primary challenge was pixel density. Current generative models limited. To four k or maybe five k resolution, meaning that in a single pass, each robot would occupy too small an area to capture the fine detail.
pairs.zh: 客户告诉我们，他们喜欢 Figma Weave 将工艺融入从复杂的构图到令人惊叹的舞台视觉效果等各个方面，从而帮助他们增强创作过程。NVIDIA 就是一个例子。在 NVIDIA CS 主题演讲中，该团队着手创建 20 个独特机器人的高保真集体照。对于巨大的 12 k Keynote 屏幕。对于如此令人难以置信的规模，他们需要灵活的工作流程。这允许快速迭代，而无需重建整个场景。主要挑战是像素密度。当前的生成模型有限。分辨率为 4 k 或 5 k，这意味着在一次通过中，每个机器人占据的区域太小而无法捕捉精细细节。

pairs.en: NVIDIA used Figma Weave to generate low fidelity three d models that locked composition and camera angles. Then they created detailed four k versions of each robot to fit the final frame. A custom AI agent also built in Figma Weave enhanced rapid lighting exploration, before the full scene was generated. Upscale to 12 k, and selectively refined for detail. The end result a cinematic Keynote visual powered by a modular AI driven workflow. In the future, we believe far more people will create across the Figma platform. Beyond the confines of traditional product development. To meet that opportunity, we're pushing the boundaries of not only what you can create in Figma, but who can create in Figma as well.
pairs.zh: NVIDIA 使用 Figma Weave 生成​​低保真度 3D 模型，锁定构图和摄像机角度。然后他们为每个机器人创建了 4 k 个详细版本以适应最终框架。Figma Weave 中还内置了自定义 AI 代理，在生成完整场景之前增强了快速照明探索。升级至 12 k，并对细节进行选择性精炼。最终结果是由模块化人工智能驱动的工作流程提供支持的电影 Keynote 视觉效果。未来，我们相信会有更多的人通过 Figma 平台进行创作。超越传统产品开发的范围。为了抓住这个机会，我们不仅突破了您可以在 Figma 中创造的界限，而且也突破了谁可以在 Figma 中创造的界限。

pairs.en: And we are accelerating into that future. AI offers a new creative starting point. It's like clay. That you can shape. The first prompt does not need to be the final output. That's where humans come in. And whether this process starts in a terminal a prompt box, with UI in the Figma Canvas, a hand drawn sketch, Figma is the place where it all comes together. Design is where all of that connects. With code and Canvas. Speed and craft, agents and human judgment. We're excited about what this means for our users and for Figma. We're focused on building the platform that makes this future possible. With that, I'll pass it to Praveen. Thanks, Dylan.
pairs.zh: 我们正在加速迈向这个未来。人工智能提供了新的创意起点。它就像粘土一样。你可以塑造。第一个提示不一定是最终输出。这就是人类发挥作用的地方。无论这个过程是从终端、提示框开始，还是 Figma Canvas 中的 UI、手绘草图，Figma 都是将这一切结合在一起的地方。设计是所有这些连接的地方。带有代码和画布。速度和技巧、代理人和人类判断。我们很高兴这对我们的用户和 Figma 意味着什么。我们专注于构建使这个未来成为可能的平台。这样，我会将其传递给 Praveen。谢谢，迪伦。

pairs.en: We're proud of how the team closed
pairs.zh: 我们为团队的关闭方式感到自豪

pairs.en: Praveer Melwani:out the year with another strong quarter punctuating a record 2025. Our total revenue in the fourth quarter was $34,000,000 growing 40% year over year and exceeding the high end of our guidance. For the full year, revenue was $1,056,000,000 up 41% year over year, also above the high end of our guidance. Sequentially, Q4 was our best quarter of net new revenue added and drove an acceleration in year over year revenue growth. Our new product launches supported both new customer acquisition and expansion, driving improvements across All of our key business metrics in Q4 compared to Q3. Our retention and expansion metrics outperformed expectations in Q4.
pairs.zh: Praveer Melwani：今年又一个强劲的季度标志着 2025 年创历史新高。我们第四季度的总收入为 34,000,000 美元，同比增长 40%，超过了我们指导的上限。For the full year, revenue was $1,056,000,000 up 41% year over year, also above the high end of our guidance.因此，第四季度是我们净新增收入最好的季度，并推动了收入同比加速增长。我们的新产品发布支持了新客户的获取和扩张，与第三季度相比，推动了第四季度我们所有关键业务指标的改进。我们的保留和扩展指标在第四季度超出了预期。

pairs.en: Our net dollar retention rate for paid customers spending more than $10,000 in ARR ended the quarter at a 136%, an increase of five percentage points quarter over quarter and our highest rate over the last ten quarters. Our gross retention rate for paid customers spending more than $10,000 in ARR remained consistent 97%, reflecting the overall durability of our customer relationships. Q4 demonstrated momentum across each of our customer tiers. We ended the quarter adding nine fifty one net customers spending more than $10,000 in ARR and 143 net customers spending more than $100,000 in ARR, growing that tier by 46% year over year.
pairs.zh: 本季度末，ARR 支出超过 10,000 美元的付费客户的净美元留存率为 136%，环比增长了 5 个百分点，是过去 10 个季度以来的最高水平。我们的 ARR 支出超过 10,000 美元的付费客户的毛保留率始终保持在 97%，这反映了我们客户关系的整体持久性。第四季度我们每个客户层都表现出了强劲的势头。截至本季度末，我们增加了 9 位 51 位 ARR 支出超过 10,000 美元的净客户，以及 143 位 ARR 支出超过 100,000 美元的净客户，该层级同比增长 46%。

pairs.en: A three percentage point acceleration Breaking down growth across the quarter and full year, a few things stand out. Relative to Q3. First, we continue to see strong expansion dynamics as our customers broaden and deepen their use of our platform driving larger renewals. We now have 67 paid customers spending more than $1,000,000 in ARR growing 68% year over year. Across tiers, customers are growing their seat counts, expanding into new functions and teams, and deepening their usage and engagement. Examples from last quarter include a hyperscaler doubling their footprint with over a quarter of new licenses going to product managers.
pairs.zh: 加速三个百分点 仔细分析整个季度和全年的增长情况，有几件事很引人注目。相对于第三季度。首先，随着客户扩大和深化对我们平台的使用，推动更大规模的续订，我们继续看到强劲的扩张动力。目前，我们有 67 名付费客户的 ARR 支出超过 1,000,000 美元，同比增长 68%。在各个层级，客户正在增加他们的席位数量，扩展到新的职能和团队，并加深他们的使用和参与。上个季度的例子包括一家超大规模企业将其足迹增加了一倍，超过四分之一的新许可证授予了产品经理。

pairs.en: A top 10 global bank embedding Figma even deeper in key engineering workflows growing dev seats by 69%, and a transatlantic airline going all in with Figma on a multiyear commitment to elevate all parts of their operations from booking and check-in experiences their loyalty platform, internal tools for crew and airport staff. The pull from our customers is real. Second, we've built deeper relationships at all levels within our customer base focused on positioning, improving Figma's value as a system of record across design and product development. We are increasingly partnering not just with design champions, but with central IT on driving the adoption of Figma's platform as a core part of their enterprise technology stacks.
pairs.zh: 一家全球十大银行将 Figma 更深入地嵌入到关键工程工作流程中，使开发人员席位增加了 69%，一家跨大西洋航空公司全力支持 Figma，致力于提升其所有运营部门的预订和登机体验、忠诚度平台、机组人员和机场工作人员的内部工具等多年承诺。来自客户的吸引力是真实的。其次，我们在客户群的各个层面建立了更深入的关系，重点关注定位，提高 Figma 作为设计和产品开发记录系统的价值。我们不仅越来越多地与设计冠军合作，而且还与中央 IT 部门合作，推动 Figma 平台的采用，将其作为其企业技术堆栈的核心部分。

pairs.en: More teams across the organization to collaborate in one platform. And we're seeing growing demand across our customer base for our Governance Plus add on as enterprises place greater emphasis on compliance, and centralized governance. Third, we continue to invest in our international business. Our international revenue grew 45% year over year. And we see meaningful runway for continued investment While international users represented approximately 85% of monthly active users, they accounted for 54% of revenue in Q4. with the most recent being our launch in India last November. Finally, customers continue to renew into our new pricing and packaging through March, which contributed a mid single digit benefit to full year 2025 revenue growth.
pairs.zh: 整个组织内的更多团队可以在一个平台上进行协作。随着企业更加重视合规性和集中治理，我们发现客户群对 Governance Plus 附加功能的需求不断增长。第三，我们继续投资国际业务。我们的国际收入同比增长 45%。我们看到了持续投资的有意义的跑道虽然国际用户约占每月活跃用户的 85%，但他们占第四季度收入的 54%。最近一次是我们去年 11 月在印度推出的。最后，截至 3 月份，客户继续更新我们的新定价和包装，这为 2025 年全年收入增长贡献了中个位数的收益。

pairs.en: Turning to some key income statement results. Unless otherwise noted, all metrics are non GAAP. We've provided a reconciliation of GAAP to non GAAP financials in our earnings release, which is posted to our website. Our gross profit for the quarter was $262,000,000 representing a gross margin of 86%. For the full year, gross profit was $931,000,000 with a gross margin of 88%. While customer adoption of Make and our AI features continued to ramp, with Make weekly active users up over 70% quarter over quarter, improvements in infrastructure optimization reduced our cost to serve each user and led to stable gross margins quarter over quarter. Our operating income for the quarter was $44,000,000 representing an operating margin of 14%.
pairs.zh: 转向一些关键的损益表结果。除非另有说明，所有指标均为非 GAAP 指标。我们在我们网站上发布的收益报告中提供了 GAAP 与非 GAAP 财务数据的调节表。我们本季度的毛利润为 262,000,000 美元，毛利率为 86%。全年毛利润为 9.31 亿美元，毛利率为 88%。虽然客户对 Make 和我们的人工智能功能的采用持续增加，Make 每周活跃用户环比增长超过 70%，但基础设施优化的改进降低了我们为每个用户提供服务的成本，并带来了环比稳定的毛利率。我们本季度的营业收入为 44,000,000 美元，营业利润率为 14%。

pairs.en: And for the full year, operating income was a $130,000,000 exceeding the high end of our guidance with an operating margin of 12%. On a year over year basis, our operating expenses increased as we invested in our people, infrastructure, and systems to support the pace of our product development and strategic growth opportunities for our business. This was offset by the outperformance we recognized in our top line and gross margin, which flowed through to our operating income. Additionally, our full year non GAAP tax rate ended at 14.5 which we expect to remain consistent throughout 2026. Our Q4 adjusted free cash flow was $38,000,000 with an adjusted free cash flow margin of 13%.
pairs.zh: 全年营业收入超过我们指导的上限 1.3 亿美元，营业利润率为 12%。与去年同期相比，我们的运营费用有所增加，因为我们对人员、基础设施和系统进行了投资，以支持我们产品开发的步伐和业务的战略增长机会。这被我们在营收和毛利率方面的优异表现所抵消，而毛利率又转化为我们的营业收入。此外，我们全年非 GAAP 税率为 14.5，我们预计整个 2026 年将保持一致。第四季度调整后自由现金流为 38,000,000 美元，调整后自由现金流利润率为 13%。

pairs.en: We ended the year with $1,700,000,000 in cash equivalents, and marketable securities on hand. As we previewed last quarter, adjusted free cash flow declined sequentially in Q4 driven by continued investment in infrastructure, and AI, changes in the timing of vendor payments, and a one time $25,000,000 IP transfer tax payment related to our acquisition of Weavie. These impacts were partially offset by strength in customer collections. We remain confident in the long term cash generating profile of the business. Before turning to our outlook, I want to briefly address stock based compensation and dilution.
pairs.zh: 到年底，我们手头有 17 亿美元的现金等价物和有价证券。正如我们上季度预测的那样，由于对基础设施和人工智能的持续投资、供应商付款时间的变化以及与我们收购 Weavie 相关的一次性 25,000,000 美元的知识产权转让税，调整后的自由现金流在第四季度连续下降。这些影响被客户群的实力所部分抵消。我们对该业务的长期现金产生能力仍然充满信心。在展望我们的前景之前，我想简要讨论一下基于股票的薪酬和稀释。

pairs.en: Stock based compensation was elevated in 2025, reflecting the recognition of expenses attributable to the IPO, performance based RSU vesting, and the launch of our employee stock purchase program and equity in connection with acquisitions. These impacts were largely one time and not reflective of our steady state compensation framework. Looking ahead, as revenue continues to scale, we expect stock based compensation as a percentage of revenue to improve. We remain committed to managing dilution responsibly. Now turning to our outlook for 2026. When we look ahead, we believe that Figma will continue to set the standard for how great products are designed and built.
pairs.zh: 基于股票的薪酬在 2025 年有所提高，反映出对 IPO 费用的确认、基于业绩的 RSU 归属、以及启动我们的员工股票购买计划和与收购相关的股权。这些影响很大程度上是一次性的，并不反映我们的稳态补偿框架。展望未来，随着收入继续扩大，我们预计基于股票的薪酬占收入的百分比将会提高。我们仍然致力于负责任地管理稀释。现在转向我们对 2026 年的展望。展望未来，我们相信 Figma 将继续为优秀产品的设计和制造树立标准。

pairs.en: We are investing deeply in the business to define new AI native workflows and better support our customers as they adapt to new ways of working. At the same time, we've always been disciplined as we scale our business with a focus on the long term. For the first quarter in 2026, we expect revenue in the range of $315,000,000 to $317,000,000 implying 38% growth at the midpoint. And for the full year, anticipate that revenue will be between 1,366,000,000.000 to $1,374,000,000 implying 30% growth at the midpoint. Our outlook reflects the sustained growth and momentum in the business that we experienced in 2025.
pairs.zh: 我们正在对业务进行深入投资，以定义新的人工智能原生工作流程，并在客户适应新的工作方式时更好地支持他们。与此同时，我们在扩展业务时始终遵守纪律，着眼于长期。对于 2026 年第一季度，我们预计收入在 315,000,000 美元至 317,000,000 美元之间，意味着中值增长 38%。预计全年收入将在 1,366,000,000,000 美元至 1,374,000,000 美元之间，这意味着中点增长 30%。我们的展望反映了我们在 2025 年经历的业务持续增长和势头。

pairs.en: Including benefits from our new products and offerings, seed expansion from new and existing customers, and international expansion. At this time last year, we had no customers consuming AI credits. Today, we're seeing approximately 75% of paid customers with over $10,000 in ARR consuming AI credits on a weekly basis, with adoption continuing to ramp. Our outlook reflects the seat adoption and usage patterns we're seeing today. We'll plan to refine our assumptions in the months ahead as we continue to both learn from customer consumption behavior and drive further AI adoption around new feature releases.
pairs.zh: 包括我们的新产品和产品带来的好处、新客户和现有客户的种子扩张以及国际扩张。去年这个时候，我们还没有客户消耗人工智能积分。如今，我们看到大约 75% 的 ARR 超过 10,000 美元的付费客户每周都会消耗 AI 积分，并且采用率持续上升。我们的前景反映了我们今天所看到的座椅采用和使用模式。我们将计划在未来几个月完善我们的假设，因为我们将继续从客户消费行为中学习并围绕新功能发布进一步推动人工智能的采用。

pairs.en: This March is when our model will shift to monetizing both seats and credits a dynamic that is not reflected in our historical revenue results. We expect our full year non GAAP operating income to be $100,000,000 and $110,000,000 this represents a non GAAP operating margin of 8% at the midpoint. In 2026, we plan to accelerate our investment in AI and inference while building a world class team and go to market motion. As a reminder, while we are not issuing quarterly operating income guidance, there is some seasonality in our operating income. Our Q2 operating income has historically been impacted by our annual user conference config, and we anticipate a similar impact in 2026.
pairs.zh: 今年三月，我们的模式将转向将席位和积分货币化，这一动态并未反映在我们的历史收入结果中。我们预计全年非 GAAP 营业收入分别为 100,000,000 美元和 110,000,000 美元，这意味着非 GAAP 营业利润率为中点 8%。2026年，我们计划加快对人工智能和推理的投资，同时建立世界一流的团队并走向市场。提醒一下，虽然我们没有发布季度营业收入指引，但我们的营业收入存在一定的季节性。我们第二季度的营业收入历来受到年度用户会议配置的影响，我们预计 2026 年也会产生类似的影响。

pairs.en: We also expect adjusted free cash flow to be relatively consistent with non GAAP operating profit for the full year. To close out, we are energized by the incredible year we had in 2025. We added a record number of new customers, exited the year with accelerating revenue growth, while focused on product velocity. But we are even more excited about what's ahead for us. Both for our customers and for the expanding capabilities on our platform. With that, I'll hand it over to the operator for Q and A. Thank you,
pairs.zh: 我们还预计调整后的自由现金流将与全年非公认会计原则营业利润相对一致。最后，我们对 2025 年令人难以置信的一年感到充满活力。我们增加了创纪录数量的新客户，以加速收入增长结束了这一年，同时专注于产品速度。但我们对未来的发展感到更加兴奋。无论是为了我们的客户还是为了我们平台上的扩展功能。这样，我就把它交给接线员进行问答。谢谢，

pairs.en: Operator:We do ask that you limit your questions to one initial and one follow-up. Once again, that is star one if you have a question. The first question comes from Arjun Bhatia William Blair. Perfect.
pairs.zh: 接线员：我们确实要求您将问题限制为一个初始问题和一个后续问题。再说一遍，如果您有问题，那就是第一颗星。第一个问题来自阿琼·巴蒂亚·威廉·布莱尔。完美的。

pairs.en: Arjun Bhatia:Thank you so much, and congrats on a very strong end of the year here. Dylan, if I can ask one just kind of philosophical question if we could step back a little bit. You know, we've had I think, a lot of noise made in the market about agentic layer offerings like Cloud CodeWork and even OpenFlow. And I'm curious your perspective on what that means just for UI UX broadly. Like, does it make it more of a differentiator Do you do you think, like, user interface gets pushed to the back as this new agentic layer emerges?
pairs.zh: Arjun Bhatia：非常感谢，并祝贺您在今年年底取得了非常好的成绩。迪伦，如果我们可以退一步，我可以问一个哲学问题吗？你知道，我认为市场上已经有很多关于 Cloud CodeWork 甚至 OpenFlow 等代理层产品的噪音。我很好奇你对这对 UI UX 的广泛意义有何看法。就像，它是否使它更像一个差异化因素，你认为，就像，随着这个新的代理层的出现，用户界面会被推到后面吗？

pairs.en: Like, what are sort of the puts and takes and the dynamics that you're thinking about as these new tools emerge?
pairs.zh: 比如，当这些新工具出现时，您正在考虑哪些看跌期权和动态？

pairs.en: Dylan Field:Yeah. Absolutely. Thank you for the question, for joining us today. So yeah, I think right now, if you're willing to hand off mission critical work to agents and just let them do it unsupervised. You know, you're a very brave person. But that joke aside, I think it is the case that humans will continue to use software increasingly agents will too. And I'm excited about that. I think that this creates more surface for designers to work with and design and think through. I will say that I think that the discourse around UI and how agents will change UI or not change UI, It is a bit extrapolating from current state.
pairs.zh: 迪伦·菲尔德：是的。绝对地。感谢您提出问题，今天加入我们。所以，是的，我想现在，如果你愿意将关键任务工作交给特工并让他们在无人监督的情况下完成。你知道，你是一个非常勇敢的人。但抛开这个笑话不谈，我认为人类将继续越来越多地使用软件，代理也会如此。我对此感到很兴奋。我认为这为设计师创造了更多的合作、设计和思考的空间。我想说的是，我认为围绕 UI 的讨论以及代理将如何改变 UI 或不改变 UI，这有点从当前状态推断。

pairs.en: Arjun Bhatia:And
pairs.zh: 阿琼·巴蒂亚：还有

pairs.en: Dylan Field:humans are really hardwired to think and process information visually, Even as agents take on more work, people will still need to understand audit trust what's happening, and that requires visual interfaces that are human readable. Lastly, I would just say that, we're going to see new interaction paradigms emerge here. And they'll have to be thoughtfully designed, adopted, For example, I think about Figma's multiplayer interface for Figma design in our Canvas. And how humans work alongside other humans, I think that we'll see, agents also working alongside humans both synchronously and asynchronously And that, again, will lead to more intentionality around how to design software and what the surfaces are you need to design in the first place.
pairs.zh: Dylan Field：人类确实天生就以视觉方式思考和处理信息，即使代理承担了更多工作，人们仍然需要了解审计信任正在发生的事情，而这需要人类可读的可视化界面。最后，我想说的是，我们将看到新的交互范式在这里出现。它们必须经过深思熟虑的设计和采用，例如，我在 Canvas 中考虑 Figma 的多人游戏界面。我想我们会看到，人类如何与其他人类一起工作，代理也可以同步和异步地与人类一起工作，这将再次导致围绕如何设计软件以及首先需要设计什么表面产生更多的意向性。

pairs.en: And a world where coding is just no longer constraint, design craft point of view, that's the differentiator. And I'm really excited for that future.
pairs.zh: 从设计工艺的角度来看，编码不再是约束的世界，这就是差异化因素。我对这个未来感到非常兴奋。

pairs.en: Arjun Bhatia:Perfect. It's very helpful color. Thank you. And then, Praveen, I had one for you. Obviously, it seems like the business is firing and also under as you had growth acceleration in Q4 and are ticked up This is all happening, before your credit consumption monetization kicks in kicks in so I'm just curious, like, what as you think about the 2026 guide, how are you benchmarking the range of outcomes from credit monetization, post, March?
pairs.zh: 阿琼·巴蒂亚：完美。这是非常有用的颜色。谢谢。然后，普拉文，我给你准备了一份。显然，随着第四季度的增长加速，业务似乎正在蓬勃发展，并且有所上升。这一切都发生在您的信贷消费货币化启动之前，所以我很好奇，就像您对 2026 年指南的看法一样，您如何对 3 月份后信贷货币化的结果范围进行基准测试？

pairs.en: Praveer Melwani:Yeah. I appreciate the question, Arjun, and good to hear from you. You know, I think if I if I take a step back for a second and just think about what we've been doing as we've embedded AI across, the entirety of our product suite. You know? Everyone from a from a user who's a starter user on our on our free plan has access to credits that we've embedded within their seats. So, you know, for some time now, we've already started to see usage ramp You know, we shared in the prepared remarks about 75% of our 10 k plus customers, today are actually consuming credits on a weekly basis.
pairs.zh: 普拉维尔·梅尔瓦尼：是的。我很高兴听到你的提问，阿琼，很高兴收到你的来信。你知道，我想如果我退后一步，想想我们在将人工智能嵌入整个产品套件时一直在做什么。你知道？我们的免费计划中的每个人都可以使用我们嵌入在他们的席位中的积分。所以，您知道，一段时间以来，我们已经开始看到使用量的增长。您知道，我们在准备好的评论中分享了我们 10,000 多名客户中大约 75% 今天实际上每周都在消耗积分。

pairs.en: And, you know, this is this is a this is an evolving number because we continue to introduce new AI features and surfaces that continue to draw an ever increasing number of credits over time. You know, we based our guidance on an understanding of, you know, current observed seed adoption behavior and usage trends. You know, we expect that to be refined as we both introduce new surfaces as well as really start to navigate that point. After we've begun to enforce our seat limits. You know, there's an opportunity here to overperform as we built confidence in the observed, usage behaviors, but we'll continue that to add additional value, to our users along the way.
pairs.zh: 而且，你知道，这是一个不断变化的数字，因为我们不断推出新的人工智能功能和界面，随着时间的推移，这些功能和界面将继续吸引越来越多的学分。您知道，我们的指导基于对当前观察到的种子采用行为和使用趋势的理解。你知道，随着我们引入新的表面并真正开始导航这一点，我们希望这一点能够得到完善。在我们开始执行座位限制之后。您知道，随着我们对观察到的使用行为建立信心，这里有机会表现出色，但我们将继续这一点，为我们的用户增加额外的价值。

pairs.en: So know, we're excited here. I think we feel we feel, you know, we've we've got some you've got a whole bunch of irons in the fire here, and, you know, we'll continue to refine our focus and story. In the coming quarters.
pairs.zh: 所以知道，我们在这里很兴奋。我想我们觉得我们感觉，你知道，我们已经有了一些你在这里有一大堆熨斗的东西，而且，你知道，我们将继续完善我们的焦点和故事。在未来几个季度。

pairs.en: Operator:The next question today comes from Michael Turrin from Wells Fargo.
pairs.zh: 接线员：今天的下一个问题来自富国银行的 Michael Turrin。

pairs.en: Michael Turrin:Hey, great. Thanks. Appreciate you making time. Dylan, I'd be remiss if I didn't start with another bigger picture question for you. The stats you're giving on May seem to be hinting at new user types.
pairs.zh: 迈克尔·特林：嘿，太棒了。谢谢。感谢您腾出时间。迪伦，如果我没有向你提出另一个更大的问题，那就是我的失职。您提供的五月份统计数据似乎暗示了新的用户类型。

pairs.en: Keith Weiss:Can you just speak to what you're seeing from customers using Make and the user types that you're seeing? I think it'd be interesting to hear you just articulate on if this could be actually seed expanding for Figma in the world or investors or concerned around seat compression in most places.
pairs.zh: Keith Weiss：您能谈谈您从使用 Make 的客户那里看到的情况以及您看到的用户类型吗？我认为听到您阐明这是否真的可以为 Figma 在世界范围内或投资者或对大多数地方的座位压缩问题进行种子扩张，会很有趣。

pairs.en: Dylan Field:Yeah. Thank you for the question, and it's definitely something that we're excited about and tracking As Praveer mentioned, in his remarks earlier, we are seeing customers that are bringing, for example, product managers into the life cycle. And, certainly, as we use Figma make just internally, but also witness with some of our customers, internal tools, which can mean all sorts of different personas. Quite interesting. So I think that overall, there's a lot of opportunity to start to reach into use cases like UX researchers and other use cases around the team as well. And with that said, I think that know, there's much more to do here. And we're excited to go do that.
pairs.zh: 迪伦·菲尔德：是的。谢谢你提出这个问题，这绝对是我们感到兴奋和跟踪的事情正如普拉维尔在他之前的评论中提到的，我们看到客户正在将产品经理等引入生命周期。当然，当我们在内部使用 Figma make 时，也会与我们的一些客户一起见证内部工具，这可能意味着各种不同的角色。很有趣。因此，我认为总的来说，有很多机会开始接触用户体验研究人员等用例以及团队周围的其他用例。话虽如此，我认为我们还有很多事情要做。我们很高兴能够做到这一点。

pairs.en: Keith Weiss:And just as a follow-up, Praveer, I've gotten some questions on just the free cash flow comment you made in terms of the guide. Are you saying dollar amounts similar to operating income? And if so, that's I think a bit lower than we were forecasting. So, just any commentary on if that's kind of make consumption based or just what the drivers and that comment were? Thank you.
pairs.zh: Keith Weiss：作为后续行动，Praver，我收到了一些关于您在指南中所做的自由现金流评论的问题。您是说美元金额与营业收入相似吗？如果是这样，我认为这比我们的预测要低一些。那么，是否有任何关于这是否是基于消费的评论，或者只是驱动因素和评论是什么？谢谢。

pairs.en: Praveer Melwani:Yeah. I mean, I think there's a few things that'll occur this year that are different from last year. And as we transition and as we continue to invest in the business, that was the spirit of the of the comment. So, you know, this will you know, this will be a full year of us serving our AI features GA'd them in the summer of last year. And as we start to see the continued ramp there, you know, the expectation on what it does to margin is what we folded through the guide.
pairs.zh: 普拉维尔·梅尔瓦尼：是的。我的意思是，我认为今年会发生一些与去年不同的事情。随着我们的转型和继续投资业务，这就是评论的精神。所以，你知道，这将是我们在去年夏天为我们的人工智能功能提供通用服务的一整年。当我们开始看到那里的持续增长时，你知道，对它对利润率的预期是我们在指南中折叠的内容。

pairs.en: You know, I think what is starting to get interesting here is as we start to introduce, you know, yet another monetization lever for us, in the in the introduction of our AI credits. It starts to create a little bit more of a natural offset there. Over time. So, you know, I think we'll we'll share more with you guys as we start to observe it. But right now, I think that's the best way to model it. Today.
pairs.zh: 你知道，我认为这里开始变得有趣的是，我们开始引入，你知道，我们的另一个货币化杠杆，在引入我们的人工智能积分时。它开始在那里产生更多的自然偏移。随着时间的推移。所以，你知道，我想当我们开始观察它时，我们会与你们分享更多。但现在，我认为这是建模的最佳方法。今天。

pairs.en: Operator:Elizabeth Porter from Morgan Stanley has the next question.
pairs.zh: 接线员：摩根士丹利的伊丽莎白·波特有下一个问题。

pairs.en: Keith Weiss:This is Keith Weiss sitting in for Elizabeth Porter. Thank you, for taking the question. And, congratulations on a spectacular and to, what was a great year for Pigma in 2025. Two questions, one for Dylan, one for Previr. For Dylan, you announced an exciting new integration with Claude and Tropic's doing a lot of great innovation. But investors are somewhat worried about kind of letting the fox into the henhouse if you will, and trying to figure out where the dividing lines are of what is parts of the equation that are gonna remain solely within kind of the Figma context and what is and profit going to be able to do.
pairs.zh: 基思·韦斯：我是基思·韦斯，代替伊丽莎白·波特。谢谢您提出问题。并且，祝贺 Pigma 取得了辉煌的成就，2025 年是伟大的一年。有两个问题，一个问 Dylan，一个问 Previr。对于 Dylan，您宣布了与 Claude 的令人兴奋的新整合，Tropic 做了很多伟大的创新。但投资者有些担心，如果你愿意的话，会有点让狐狸进鸡舍，并试图找出方程式中哪些部分将仅保留在 Figma 背景下的分界线，以及将能够做什么和利润。

pairs.en: You guys like, how do you guys see that question? Like, how do you see the dividing line between what is in the wheel well of Sigma and is always gonna be in the wheel well of Sigma versus what, clot or what entropic brings to the equation. And then for Previr, on the price increase, I'm getting a lot of questions from investors in terms of understand mid single digit impact for the full year. Can you give us any sense of how that ramped in Q3 four and what type of contribution it had in Q4?
pairs.zh: 各位，你们怎么看这个问题呢？比如，你如何看待 Sigma 轮舱中的东西和 Sigma 轮舱中永远存在的东西与方程式中带来的凝块或熵之间的分界线。然后，对于 Previr，关于价格上涨，我收到了投资者的很多问题，要求了解全年中个位数的影响。您能否告诉我们第三季度第四季度的增长情况以及第四季度的贡献类型？

pairs.en: And how we should think about the pricing increase contribution to Q1 so we could better model kind of the slope for the coming year.
pairs.zh: 我们应该如何考虑第一季度的定价增长贡献，以便我们可以更好地为来年的斜率建模。

pairs.en: Dylan Field:Yeah. Thank you for the questions, and I'll I'll start off with the first one. I would just say first that one thing that is just interesting is how virtually every frontier lab is using Figma to design how they bring their models to users and shape their product surfaces. And they are also been great partners with us. So you know, that has been just interesting to see firsthand. I think zooming out, our AI strategy is pretty simple. We always want to be in a place where as models get better, Figma gets better. And know, easy to say, but have to make sure that is the case for everything we do.
pairs.zh: 迪伦·菲尔德：是的。谢谢大家的提问，我将从第一个问题开始。我首先要说的是，一件有趣的事情是，几乎每个前沿实验室都在使用 Figma 来设计如何将模型呈现给用户并塑造产品表面。他们也是我们很好的合作伙伴。所以你知道，亲眼目睹这一点很有趣。我认为缩小范围，我们的人工智能策略非常简单。我们总是希望随着模型变得更好，Figma 也会变得更好。说起来容易，但必须确保我们所做的一切都是如此。

pairs.en: And also that we can have an edge. And I think that one thing that answers your questions directly around sort of where are the lines between is I would focus on tasks which are more verifiable versus nonverifiable. Design is inherently nonverifiable. And I think that's why you're gonna see humans in the loop and even more focus on design as well. Because as code, becomes something that more people can do, with the assistance of models, the value will move up the stack. And with the value moving up the stack, I believe we're going to see even greater focus on design.
pairs.zh: 而且我们可以拥有优势。我认为直接回答你的问题的一件事是，我会关注那些更可验证而不是不可验证的任务。设计本质上是不可验证的。我认为这就是为什么你会看到人类参与其中，甚至更加关注设计。因为当代码成为更多人可以做的事情时，在模型的帮助下，价值就会向上移动。随着价值的提升，我相信我们将看到对设计的更多关注。

pairs.en: And we're already seeing it, I think, with the hires people are making and the ways that non designers are getting involved, the design process.
pairs.zh: 我认为，通过人们的招聘以及非设计师参与设计过程的方式，我们已经看到了这一点。

pairs.en: Operator:Career?
pairs.zh: 接线员：职业？

pairs.en: Praveer Melwani:And, you know, I'll answer your question on pricing and packaging there. So, you know, just as a reminder, about three quarters out of the initial rollout of the changes that we implemented in March. So, you know, the way that is gonna track to revenue as folks are filtering in through their renewals, have a you have a growing benefit there. Over the course of the year. We lapsed that anniversary in March, and then you'll start to you'll start to see a winning benefit, towards the back half of this year.
pairs.zh: Praveer Melwani：而且，你知道，我将在那里回答你有关定价和包装的问题。所以，提醒一下，我们在 3 月份实施的变更初步推出已经进行了大约四分之三。所以，你知道，随着人们通过续订渗透进来，你将获得越来越多的收益，从而追踪收入。一年来。我们在三月份度过了这个周年纪念日，然后您将开始看到今年下半年的成功效益。

pairs.en: So, it's a it's a little bit like a bell curve there where, you know, a it grows over it grows over, you know, the first four quarters, then it'll wane over the next
pairs.zh: 所以，这有点像钟形曲线，你知道，它会增长，你知道，前四个季度，然后它会在下一个季度减弱

pairs.en: Keith Weiss:four.
pairs.zh: 基思·韦斯：四。

pairs.en: Michael Turrin:Excellent. Thank you, guys.
pairs.zh: 迈克尔·特林：非常好。谢谢你们，伙计们。

pairs.en: Operator:Gabriela Borges from Goldman Sachs has the next question. Hi. Good afternoon. Thank you. I want to follow-up on the conversation on Sigma make and ask specifically about some of the competition that you're seeing in the prototyping space. Give us a sense of what you're seeing in terms of perhaps consolidating budget within your customers? Away from other prototyping solutions. But are you still seeing fragmentation in that world where customers maybe use multiple tools for prototyping and then them into signal when they're when they're ready to go to the next phase of the design process. Thank you.
pairs.zh: 接线员：高盛的 Gabriela Borges 有下一个问题。你好。下午好。谢谢。我想跟进有关 Sigma make 的对话，并具体询问您在原型设计领域看到的一些竞争情况。请让我们了解一下您在整合客户预算方面所看到的情况？远离其他原型解决方案。但是，您是否仍然看到那个世界的碎片化，客户可能使用多种工具进行原型设计，然后在准备好进入设计过程的下一阶段时将它们转化为信号。谢谢。

pairs.en: Dylan Field:Yeah. Thanks for the question. I think that some of the broad strokes we're seeing are really around the power of using Figma Make alongside Figma Design And you know, one stat that we shared in the earnings call is how over 80% of full seat users of Make are also using design. And so I think that is an area that as we look ahead, we're really excited to lean into more. Is to really try to unite these surfaces better We've seen it already with things like copy layers, from Figma make into Figma design. Or taking embeds from Figma Make and putting them into Figma Design. But this is just the start.
pairs.zh: 迪伦·菲尔德：是的。谢谢你的提问。我认为我们看到的一些大纲实际上是围绕使用 Figma Make 和 Figma Design 的力量而设计的。您知道，我们在财报电话会议中分享的一项统计数据是，超过 80% 的 Make 全席用户也在使用设计。所以我认为，当我们展望未来时，我们真的很高兴能够深入研究这个领域。是真正尝试更好地结合这些表面，我们已经在复制图层等方面看到了这一点，从 Figma make 到 Figma design。或者从 Figma Make 中嵌入并放入 Figma Design 中。但这只是开始。

pairs.en: I think that there's so much more we can do here And a big part of the platform differentiation we'll have come from the unification of the surfaces. I also think that it's really important to remember that the round tripping between code and design can really set us apart here. And I am just very bullish on the opportunity that could exist without round trip.
pairs.zh: 我认为我们在这里可以做的还有很多，我们平台差异化的很大一部分来自于界面的统一。我还认为，记住代码和设计之间的往返确实可以让我们与众不同，这一点非常重要。我非常看好无需往返即可存在的机会。

pairs.en: Operator:Yeah. That all make sense. Thank you. And a follow-up either for yourself, Dylan, also Previr. I'm curious the budget implications. When you see a cost of our go all in on Sigma, what does that mean for how they're thinking about labor resourcing versus software resourcing? One of the things we've noticed as well you put more powerful engineering type design tools into the hands of designers who may not know how to code. So how does the mix change between design focused designers versus engineering focused designers, if that makes sense? Would just love to hear your observations broadly on implications of sigma adoption for a customer design and labor budget.
pairs.zh: 接线员：是的。这一切都有道理。谢谢。迪伦（Dylan）和普雷维尔（Previr）都可以为自己做后续工作。我很好奇预算的影响。当您看到我们全力投入 Sigma 的成本时，这对于他们如何考虑劳动力资源与软件资源意味着什么？我们还注意到的一件事是，您将更强大的工程类型设计工具交给了可能不知道如何编码的设计师手中。那么，如果有意义的话，专注于设计的设计师与专注于工程的设计师之间的混合如何变化？我很想听听您对 sigma 采用对客户设计和劳动力预算的影响的广泛观察。

pairs.en: Dylan Field:Yeah. I'll start first. I think that you know, some people are starting to call themselves design engineers. We've seen that in the past as well. Know, there's been sort of surges around even eight, nine years ago. People getting really excited about calling themselves design engineers. Kind of it went back to product design, and now that term is coming up again. I would say, overall, we're not really seeing the roles blurring more the response boring. Between roles, not just between design engineering and design, but the roles more broadly, you know, as you go up to the product design life cycle, you know, PM, engineering, design, research, and marketing even. Responsibilities are starting to blur.
pairs.zh: 迪伦·菲尔德：是的。我先开始吧。我想你知道，有些人开始称自己为设计工程师。我们过去也见过这种情况。要知道，甚至在八、九年前就出现过某种程度的激增。人们对称自己为设计工程师感到非常兴奋。有点回到了产品设计，现在这个术语又出现了。我想说，总的来说，我们并没有真正看到角色变得模糊，反应变得无聊。角色之间，不仅仅是设计工程和设计之间，而是更广泛的角色之间，你知道，当你进入产品设计生命周期时，你知道，PM、工程、设计、研究和营销。责任开始模糊。

pairs.en: People are feeling the need to be more generalist, and so I think that it's it's hard to split in a very accurate way because so many people that are nondesigners by title are starting to really engage with design tasks. But I'll hand it to Praveer to speak more.
pairs.zh: 人们感觉需要更加通才，所以我认为很难以非常准确的方式进行划分，因为很多非设计师的人开始真正参与设计任务。但我会把它交给普拉维尔说更多。

pairs.en: Praveer Melwani:Yeah. And then I think, you know, as it relates to the sales process and who we're having conversations with, I think we've, you know, over time, elevated the conversations to, you know, to IT that gives us access to broader budget. You know? And when you when you then kinda look at the types of users that we're we're bringing in, there was a few that we spoke about in the prepared remarks that, you know, the ones that stand up or rather the one that stands out to me is the hyperscaler, that doubled their footprint. With about a quarter of their new seats going to product managers. And this is not a, you know, unique instance.
pairs.zh: 普拉维尔·梅尔瓦尼：是的。然后我想，你知道，因为它与销售流程以及我们正在与谁对话有关，我认为随着时间的推移，我们已经将对话提升到了 IT 领域，这使我们能够获得更广泛的预算。你知道？当你看看我们引入的用户类型时，我们在准备好的评论中谈到了一些用户类型，你知道，那些站起来的，或者更确切地说，对我来说脱颖而出的是超大规模用户，它们的足迹增加了一倍。大约四分之一的新席位给了产品经理。你知道，这并不是一个独特的例子。

pairs.en: We're starting to see this happen more and more so throughout the customer base. And so our expectation is that you know, we're pitching wider, we're pitching broader. And it's the overall platform that's ultimately winning here.
pairs.zh: 我们开始看到这种情况在整个客户群中越来越多地发生。所以我们的期望是，你知道，我们正在扩大范围，我们正在扩大范围。最终获胜的是整体平台。

pairs.en: Operator:Next up is Rishi Jaluria from RBC.
pairs.zh: 接线员：下一位是来自 RBC 的 Rishi Jaluria。

pairs.en: Rishi Jaluria:Oh, wonderful. Thanks so much for taking my question.
pairs.zh: Rishi Jaluria：哦，太棒了。非常感谢您提出我的问题。

pairs.en: Dylan Field:Maybe two, if I may. First, for Dylan.
pairs.zh: 迪伦·菲尔德：如果可以的话，也许两个。首先是迪伦。

pairs.en: Rishi Jaluria:You know, just kind of expanding on some of the dialogue that you know, you have mentioned that a lot of kind of next gen AI companies and AI labs are working with you, and you've talked about some of the MCP integrations you have with Anthropolog. And others. How do we think about the opportunity for you to, over time, start building out more formal partnerships with having know, maybe even deeper integrations or, you know, kind of joint product development, maybe if that's getting a little far. Maybe just help us understand what does that potential partnership opportunity between you and some of these other AI natives look like in terms of enabling success in your customer base?
pairs.zh: Rishi Jaluria：您知道，只是扩展了您所知道的一些对话，您提到了很多下一代人工智能公司和人工智能实验室正在与您合作，并且您谈到了与 Anthropolog 的一些 MCP 集成。还有其他人。随着时间的推移，我们如何看待您的机会，开始建立更正式的合作伙伴关系，甚至更深层次的集成，或者，您知道，联合产品开发，也许这有点遥远。也许只是帮助我们了解您和其他一些人工智能原住民之间的潜在合作机会在您的客户群中取得成功方面是什么样的？

pairs.en: Then I've got a quick follow-up for Praveer.
pairs.zh: 然后我对普拉维尔进行了快速跟进。

pairs.en: Rishi Jaluria:Sure. So
pairs.zh: Rishi Jaluria：当然。所以

pairs.en: Dylan Field:I think that right now, as we think about our core with design, And just the opportunities that models provide as they advance with code that is a big part of our focus. And
pairs.zh: Dylan Field：我认为现在，当我们思考设计的核心时，以及模型随着代码的发展而提供的机会，这是我们关注的一个重要部分。和

pairs.en: Praveer Melwani:the
pairs.zh: 普拉维尔·梅尔瓦尼：

pairs.en: Dylan Field:opportunity to partner deeply and to really make sure that we are thinking in the right ways about how models will continue to have new capabilities in the future. And then to make sure that we are, accounting for that in our product road map, as well as working closely and integrating well with various model providers is something that we paid a lot of attention to. I think going forward, as we think about some of the things we've seen with you know, for example, Figma's apps on ChatGPT, well as Claude. There is definitely stuff that we'll explore there as well.
pairs.zh: Dylan Field：深度合作的机会，并真正确保我们以正确的方式思考模型如何在未来继续拥有新功能。然后，为了确保我们做到这一点，在我们的产品路线图中考虑到这一点，以及与各种模型提供商密切合作和良好集成，这是我们非常关注的事情。我认为展望未来，当我们思考我们所看到的一些事情时，例如 ChatGPT 上的 Figma 应用程序以及 Claude。我们肯定也会在那里探索一些东西。

pairs.en: But I think it is maybe secondary to the primary objective of just having that amazing ability to work and go from code to Canvas and Canvas to code and back again That round trip is what we're really focused on. And making sure that we are always meeting the criteria of as models get better, we're getting better.
pairs.zh: 但我认为，对于主要目标来说，这可能是次要的，即拥有惊人的工作能力，从代码到画布，再从画布到代码，然后再返回，这种往返才是我们真正关注的。并确保我们始终满足模型变得更好，我们也变得更好的标准。

pairs.en: Rishi Jaluria:Alright. Very helpful. And then, Praveen, you in your prepared remarks, you closed out by talking about March is kind of the, when we're gonna start seeing this more mix between, subscription and consumption, more of a hybrid It's very consistent with what you've talked about before, and know, how the landscape seems to be shifting. Maybe as we think about, you know, that mix, two pieces.
pairs.zh: Rishi Jaluria：好的。非常有帮助。然后，Praveen，您在准备好的发言中最后谈到了 3 月，我们将开始看到订阅和消费之间更多的混合，更多的是一种混合，这与您之前谈到的非常一致，并且知道，情况似乎正在发生变化。也许正如我们所想的，你知道，那种混合，两部分。

pairs.en: Number one, how do you expect, given some of the early traction you're having, with your AI native SKUs, how do you expect that mix to shape over time And then alongside that, how do you know, what tools in your arsenal do you have to at least have visibility and predictability into future revenue so the model itself doesn't become overly volatile as we navigate through this change. Thanks so much.
pairs.zh: 第一，考虑到您对人工智能原生 SKU 的一些早期吸引力，您如何期望随着时间的推移，这种组合会如何形成？除此之外，您如何知道您的武器库中至少需要哪些工具来对未来收入具有可见性和可预测性，以便在我们经历这一变化时模型本身不会变得过于不稳定。非常感谢。

pairs.en: Praveer Melwani:Yeah. No. I appreciate the question, Rashid. You know, I think the way that, that I'd frame it is, you know, we've already started studying this usage and utilization behavior. You know? And, you know, we're prepping for this hybrid model for some time. You know, we basically we basically have telemetry into, you know, the overall the overall seat rather, the overall credit consumption on a on a per seat basis. And what we've observed is it tends to be, you know, a parallel distribution where subset of users within an organization are receiving outside value and as such are going over the, you know, projected limits that we intend to enforce.
pairs.zh: 普拉维尔·梅尔瓦尼：是的。不，我很欣赏这个问题，拉希德。你知道，我认为我所构建的方式是，你知道，我们已经开始研究这种使用和利用行为。你知道？而且，您知道，我们正在为这种混合模型做准备一段时间。你知道，我们基本上对整个座位进行遥测，而不是按每个座位的整体信用消费。我们观察到，它往往是一种并行分布，组织内的用户子集正在接收外部价值，因此超出了我们打算强制执行的预计限制。

pairs.en: Now, you know, our expectation is that we'll will continue to evolve as we introduce more servers for surfaces for folks to be able to draw down credits. We'll create more opportunities for us to, you know, have continue to shift that distribution further and further right. The way that our AI add ons are structured, they're they're structured as, you know, additional seat or additional consumption packs in addition to providing folks the opportunity to pay as you go.
pairs.zh: 现在，您知道，我们的期望是我们将继续发展，因为我们引入了更多的表面服务器，以便人们能够提取积分。我们将创造更多的机会，让我们继续将这种分布越来越右移。我们的人工智能附加组件的结构方式，除了为人们提供即用即付的机会之外，它们的结构还包括额外的座位或额外的消费包。

pairs.en: So, in the instance that you're purchasing an add on pack, you know, it co terms with your and we have more predictability in that And on the pay as you go side, you know, that is more meant to be for burst type activity. So, ultimately, it'll be a mix. We're studying it very closely, and I think, know, as we get post our the actual monetization date is when we'll is when we'll be able to further refine our assumptions.
pairs.zh: 因此，在您购买附加包的情况下，您知道，它与您的情况一致，我们在这方面具有更多的可预测性，并且在随用随付方面，您知道，这更适合突发类型的活动。所以，最终，这将是一个混合体。我们正在非常仔细地研究它，我想，当我们发布实际的货币化日期时，我们将能够进一步完善我们的假设。

pairs.en: Operator:Your next question comes from Billy Fitzsimmons from Piper Sandler.
pairs.zh: 接线员：您的下一个问题来自 Piper Sandler 的 Billy Fitzsimmons。

pairs.en: Rishi Jaluria:Hey, guys. Thanks for taking my question. The FY 2025 or the FY '26, I should say, revenue starting point, was well above expectations. If I look at the operating income guidance, midpoint implies, I think, 7.7% non GAAP operating margins compared to, I think, 12.3% in 2025. Just as we think about the drivers of this, what's kind of the breakdown between expected gross margin compression from AI investments in the AI product ramp versus
pairs.zh: Rishi Jaluria：嘿，伙计们。感谢您提出我的问题。我应该说，2025 财年或 26 财年的收入起点远高于预期。如果我看一下营业收入指引，我认为中点意味着非 GAAP 营业利润率为 7.7%，而 2025 年则为 12.3%。正如我们思考其驱动因素一样，人工智能产品增长中的人工智能投资的预期毛利率压缩与人工智能产品增长的预期毛利率压缩之间的细分是什么？

pairs.en: Mark Murphy:some incremental investments you plan on making on the OpEx line? And I know you don't guide to it, but any directional commentary on
pairs.zh: 马克·墨菲：您计划在运营支出线上进行一些增量投资？我知道你不会引导它，但任何定向评论

pairs.en: Rishi Jaluria:kind of gross margins in 2026 would be helpful.
pairs.zh: Rishi Jaluria：2026 年的毛利率会有所帮助。

pairs.en: Praveer Melwani:Yeah.
